overall_summary: elizaOS is undergoing a significant transition to v2.0.0, characterized by major database refactoring and the integration of on-chain identity via the SAID Protocol. Development focus has intensified on performance optimization and expanding the plugin ecosystem, including new tools for exchange listings and NFT capabilities.
key_facts: Milady announced the AGENTS NFT drop (ERC-8004) across ETH, BSC, Base, and Solana, providing free mints for elizaOS holders.
PR #6521 was submitted for elizaOS v2.0.0, removing 2,600 lines of code primarily to eliminate auto-migration from 1.4.x to 1.6.x.
The SAID Protocol was integrated in PR #6510 to provide agents with verifiable on-chain digital identities on the Solana blockchain.
Odilitime reported hitting 200k token limits regularly due to plugin density and initiated work to improve bootstrap providers and evaluations.
The SolCex Exchange plugin (@buzzbd/plugin-solcex-bd) for v2.0.0 was published on npm, featuring a 100-point token scoring system.
Core developers confirmed that v2.0.0 is the recommended branch, despite having less documentation than stable versions.
The project clarified the ai16z token as deprecated following a 1:6 migration to the elizaOS ecosystem.
ElizaCloud users identified critical UI bugs, including broken mousepad scrolling on the API explorer page and recurring API key errors.
open_questions: Isn't the upgrade for $milady.ai not ElizaOS?
Can we deploy dApps to Solana seeker?
Is there still a live Eliza agent that can answer questions about the most recent ElizaOS codebase?
Does 'use a different key' mean users can enter their own API keys (like OpenAI key for gpt-4o) similar to OpenRouter BYOK?
For Milady, we have BNB and Solana - are both valid?

twitter_news_highlights.claim: Shaw shares AI and development updates exclusively on Twitter, while crypto content is posted to Farcaster.

new_issues_prs.title: align RLS isolation with v1 patterns
new_issues_prs.author: standujar
new_issues_prs.significance: Critical database maintenance for v2.0.0 that defines migration strategy and security protocols.

new_issues_prs.title: add SAID Protocol on-chain Solana identity
new_issues_prs.author: kaiclawd
new_issues_prs.significance: Introduces verifiable decentralized identity for agents.

new_issues_prs.title: URL in message triggers duplicate LLM calls
new_issues_prs.author: thewoweffect
new_issues_prs.significance: Major cost and performance bug leading to doubled token usage.

overall_focus.claim: Development is centered on the transition to v2.0.0, involving significant code deletions of legacy migration paths and optimizing the runtime for multi-language support (Rust, Python, TS).

discord_updates.summary: Technical deep dive into the 'Great DB Refactor' and v2.0.0 migration strategy. Decision made to force users to move to 1.6.x before upgrading to v2.0.0.
Announcement of the SolCex Exchange plugin and discussions on ERC-8004 NFT standard implementations on Base and Ethereum.
Community showcase of versatile agents (Reservation, Travel, DB analysis) and clarification on the non-relationship between Spartan infrastructure and Milady wallet.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users reported unusable scrolling on the ElizaCloud API explorer dashboard and errors when attempting to use the 'Send Request' test feature.
Positive feedback regarding the Erlang-based 'unbreakable agents' code proposed for integration.

strategic_insights.insight: There is a temporary strategic tension between the stability of v1.x and the recommended adoption of v2.0.0, which currently lacks comprehensive documentation.
implications_or_questions: Will the lack of documentation stifle the v2 beta onboarding?
How quickly can the documentation team sync with the v2.0.0 feature set?

strategic_insights.insight: Weak communication has been identified as a source of negative sentiment among influential partner groups, coinciding with the restoration of key social media accounts.
implications_or_questions: Should a dedicated partner-comms lead be appointed to manage influential groups?

market_analysis.observation: elizaOS market cap is approximately 10 million, significantly lower than its historical peak of nearly 3 billion.
market_analysis.relevance: Reflects the transition from a hype-driven phase to an infrastructure-building phase.
